heading: Operation of Office; House privileges; compensation, allowances, and benefits; privileges and immunities; voting in committee
label: 1715 Operation of Office; House privileges; compensation, allowances, and benefits; privileges and immunities; voting in committee
text: The Delegate from Guam and the Delegate from the Virgin Islands shall have such privileges in the House of Representatives as may be afforded him under the Rules of the House of Representatives. Until the Rules of the House of Representatives are amended to provide otherwise, the Delegate from each territory shall receive the same compensation, allowances, and benefits as a Member of the House of Representatives, and shall be entitled to whatever privileges and immunities are, or hereinafter may be, granted to the Resident Commissioner for Puerto Rico: , That the right to vote in committee shall be as provided by the Rules of the House of Representatives.Provided
notes: Amendments1996— struck out last two provisos which read as follows: “, That the clerk hire allowance of each Delegate shall be a single per annum gross rate that is 60 per centum of the clerk hire allowance of a Member: , That the transportation expenses of each Delegate that are subject to reimbursement under shall not exceed the cost of four round trips each year.”Pub. L. 104–186section 43b of title 2Provided furtherProvided further
